questions.text: The sum of all interior angles of a quadrilateral is:
A simple closed curve made up of only line segments is called a:
questions.explanation: Polygons are classified by their number of sides.

questions.text: How many diagonals does a convex quadrilateral have?
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral connects opposite vertices with two diagonals.

questions.text: The sum of the measures of the external angles of any polygon is:
questions.explanation: No matter the number of sides, the exterior angles always sum to 360°.

questions.text: A quadrilateral with exactly one pair of parallel sides is a:
questions.explanation: This is the definition of a trapezium.

questions.text: In a parallelogram, opposite angles are ________.
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of parallelograms.

questions.text: A rhombus is a parallelogram with ________ sides equal.
questions.explanation: All four sides of a rhombus are equal in length.

questions.text: The diagonals of a rectangle are ________.
questions.explanation: Rectangles have diagonals of equal length.

questions.text: Which quadrilateral has all sides equal and all angles equal to 90°?
questions.explanation: A square is a regular quadrilateral.

questions.text: The diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at:
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular bisectors of each other.

questions.text: Find the number of sides of a regular polygon whose each exterior angle has a measure of 45°.
questions.explanation: Number of sides = 360 / exterior angle = 360 / 45 = 8.

questions.text: A polygon with 10 sides is called a:
questions.explanation: Deca means ten.

questions.text: Which of the following is a convex polygon?
options: A polygon where all diagonals lie inside
A polygon with one interior angle > 180°
questions.explanation: In convex polygons, no part of the diagonals lies in the exterior.

questions.text: The adjacent angles of a parallelogram are ________.
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles sum to 180° because they are consecutive interior angles.

questions.text: A quadrilateral with two pairs of equal adjacent sides but unequal opposite sides is a:
questions.explanation: A kite has exactly two pairs of equal consecutive sides.

questions.text: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon?
The minimum interior angle possible for a regular polygon is:
questions.explanation: This occurs in an equilateral triangle (3 sides).

questions.text: The maximum exterior angle possible for a regular polygon is:
questions.explanation: For an equilateral triangle, exterior angle = 180 - 60 = 120°.

questions.text: If three angles of a quadrilateral are 75°, 90°, and 75°, the fourth angle is:
Which property is NOT true for a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Diagonals are equal in rectangles and squares, but not necessarily in all parallelograms.

questions.text: A quadrilateral whose all sides are equal but angles are not 90° is a:
questions.explanation: In a rhombus, sides are equal, but angles can be any value (with opposite angles equal).

questions.text: The diagonals of which quadrilateral bisect each other at right angles?
questions.explanation: Rhombus and Square have this property.

questions.text: In a parallelogram ABCD, if ∠A = 70°, then ∠B is:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles are supplementary: 180 - 70 = 110°.

questions.text: A regular polygon has ________ sides and ________ angles equal.
questions.explanation: Regular polygons are both equilateral and equiangular.

questions.text: How many sides does a heptagon have?
questions.explanation: Hepta refers to seven.

questions.text: Which of the following quadrilaterals is NOT a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: A trapezium has only one pair of parallel sides, whereas a parallelogram needs two.

questions.text: The angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 1:2:3:4. The largest angle is:
In a kite, how many diagonals are there?
questions.explanation: All quadrilaterals have 2 diagonals.

questions.text: If each interior angle of a regular polygon is 108°, it is a:
Which of the following quadrilaterals has perpendicular diagonals?
questions.explanation: In a kite, diagonals are perpendicular to each other.

questions.text: A quadrilateral with all sides equal is necessarily a square.
options: Only if it is a polygon
questions.explanation: It could be a rhombus.

questions.text: The measure of each angle of a square is:
questions.explanation: A square is a special case of a rectangle.

questions.text: If diagonals of a parallelogram are equal, it is a ________.
questions.explanation: This is a key property that distinguishes a rectangle from a general parallelogram.

questions.text: A polygon where one or more interior angles are greater than 180° is:
questions.explanation: This allows part of a diagonal to fall outside the polygon.

questions.text: Total number of exterior angles in a pentagon is:
questions.explanation: One exterior angle for each vertex.

questions.text: Two adjacent angles of a parallelogram are (3x - 4)° and (3x + 10)°. Find x.
Diagonals of which figure do NOT bisect each other?
questions.explanation: In a non-isosceles trapezium, diagonals do not bisect each other.

questions.text: A square is both a rectangle and a rhombus.
questions.explanation: A square has properties of both: 90° angles (rectangle) and equal sides (rhombus).

questions.text: What is the sum of exterior angles of a decagon?
questions.explanation: Sum of exterior angles is 360° for any polygon.

questions.text: If a quadrilateral has both pairs of opposite sides equal, it is a ________.
questions.explanation: This is a sufficient condition for a quadrilateral to be a parallelogram.
